items.title: Jeff Teague REACTS to Rich Paul saying Scottie Pippen was MORE IMPACTFUL than Michael Jordan
items.author: Club 520 Podcast
items.body_excerpt: Jeff Teague, DJ Wells, and B Hen react to Rich Paul saying that Scottie Pippen was MORE IMPACTFUL than Michael Jordan during the Chicago Bulls’ run of six NBA championships. Jeff talks about how the Bulls had the same record the year MJ “retired” to play baseball, and that MJ…

items.title: June 2, 1989: Scottie Pippen falls to the floor after taking an elbow to the head, then is dragged off the floor by referee Joey Crawford while play continues in Game 6 of the Eastern Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 2, 1989: Scottie Pippen falls to the floor after taking an elbow to the head, then is dragged off the floor by referee Joey Crawford while play continues in Game 6 of the Eastern Conference Finals.

The Pistons defeated the Bulls, 103-94, and advanced to the NBA Finals.

items.title: Rich Paul and How Scottie Pippen Became a GOAT Debate Weapon
items.author: House of Strauss
items.body_excerpt: I apologize if Rich Paul and Max Kellerman have a good podcast if you listen to its entirety. My experience of their pod is exclusively through clips that go negatively viral because they sound like crazy people. It’s not that Paul and Kellerman come off as randomly crazy,…

items.title: May 31, 1998: Chicago's Scottie Pippen drives and scores with two minutes to go in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference Finals against Indiana.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 31, 1998: Chicago's Scottie Pippen drives and scores with two minutes to go in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference Finals against Indiana.

Pippen had 17 PTS/12 REB/2 STL and Michael Jordan had 28 PTS/9 REB/8 AST in the Bulls' 88-83 win. The Pacers' Reggie Miller scored 22 PTS.

items.title: May 30, 2000: Portland's Scottie Pippen finishes with 22 PTS (8-12 FG)/6 REB/6 STL/4 BLK in a 96-88 win over the Lakers in Game 5 of the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 30, 2000: Portland's Scottie Pippen finishes with 22 PTS (8-12 FG)/6 REB/6 STL/4 BLK in a 96-88 win over the Lakers in Game 5 of the Western Conference Finals.

Rasheed Wallace added 22 PTS/10 REB for the Blazers. Shaquille O'Neal had 31 PTS/21 REB/2 BLK for Los Angeles.

items.title: Jamal Murray makes his first All-NBA team. Scottie Barnes snubbed. Jalen Johnson didn’t have an overall better year than him imo. Luka and Cade becoming eligible hurt Barnes, Jaylen Brown (second team instead of first) and KAT, amongst others.
items.author: Wolstat
items.body_excerpt:
Jamal Murray makes his first All-NBA team. Scottie Barnes snubbed. Jalen Johnson didn’t have an overall better year than him imo. Luka and Cade becoming eligible hurt Barnes, Jaylen Brown (second team instead of first) and KAT, amongst others. 
No LeBron for first time in decades

items.title: Scottie Barnes did not make an All-NBA team.
items.author: Blake Murphy
items.body_excerpt:
Scottie Barnes did not make an All-NBA team.

He finished 18th in voting with 9 Third-Team votes.

items.title: Scottie Barnes named to second all-defensive NBA team, just third Raptors (Leonard, 2018; OG 2023) to ever make it. He finished sixth overall in voting with 42 first team votes, 46 second team votes and a mystifying 12 voters left him off ballots completely.
items.author: Doug Smith
items.body_excerpt: Scottie Barnes named to second all-defensive NBA team, just third Raptors (Leonard, 2018; OG 2023) to ever make it. He finished sixth overall in voting with 42 first team votes, 46 second team votes and a mystifying 12 voters left him off ballots completely.

items.title: Scottie Barnes has been named to All-Defense Second Team
items.author: Blake Murphy
items.body_excerpt:
Scottie Barnes has been named to All-Defense Second Team

Barnes finished sixth in total voting points, and just missed First Team
